greet.text: Gentry hasn't slept and it becomes him horribly — he's incandescent, wired to his own rig and the Count's tap both. "Slick. Good. Sit. TRODES." He steadies himself against the bench. "Tonight we stop measuring the box and we READ it. I go in through the tap. You hold my line and you do not let go, whatever you see, whatever I say, whatever SAYS ME BACK."

options.text: "First tell me what you think it is. All of it."
"And if something goes wrong in there?"

wrong.text: "Then you PULL, and I come out swearing, and we try again tomorrow." He straps in, fingers steady with pure purpose. "But it won't go wrong. The thing in there isn't hostile, Slick — I've watched its traffic for a week. It isn't defending. It's BUILDING. Hostility is a wall. This is... nesting." The word visibly bothers him. "Theory first. You've earned it."

theory.text: "The aleph is an approximation of everything — but approximations need an APPROXIMATOR." Gentry's eyes reflect his own screens like twin grids. "When the matrix Changed — the night the cowboys' gods were born — something total existed for one moment, then broke itself into the loa. I always believed the Shape shattered. But suppose a PIECE was saved. Folded down. Bought, sold, smuggled — and finally wired to a dying cowboy who's using the last of himself to finish it into a WORLD." He turns to the stretcher, almost tender. "The Count isn't dreaming, Slick. He's TERRAFORMING. Now jack in and hold my line."

options.text: "...Trodes on. Let's go meet the neighbor." (leave — jack in)

bye.text: Gentry checks your straps himself, an unprecedented courtesy, and settles into his own rig with the terrible serenity of a man about to read the book he's spent his life proving exists.
